PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Oct-28-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Neil Rones for (petitioner) Yvonne Ennon-Knighten.

The Court asked counsel which will are they asking to have admitted. Mr. Rones replied that both were written by decedent at the same time and are duplicate originals, that the decedent wrote two in case one was lost, that they are not aware of any other wills, that there is an additional witness (decedents granddaughter), that they would like to file a supplemental declaration, that the examiner thought that maybe the wills were not both written by decedent, that they will try and determine which will was written second and probate that one. The Court asked if they will be asking the Court to make a recommendation on the will, that they are identical, asked what is gained by not choosing one now and allowed counsel a moment to confer with his client. Mr. Rones stated that they ask that the attachment 4, exhibit B, be the will that is accepted and that it was filed on 10/11/16. The Court confirmed that the decision will be based on the will that was filed on 10/11/16 and granted the petition for probate of will and for letters testamentary (subject to receipt/review of the order).

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Sep-19-2016 Continued To Oct-17-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Neil Rones for and with (petitioner) Yvonne Ennon-Knighten.

Mr. Rones reported that he signed on at the end of last week, that he has not reviewed the examiner's notes and will need a continuance. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary was continued to October 17, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to respond to the examiner's notes and any other outstanding issues.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Aug-29-2016 Continued To Sep-19-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Yvonne Ennon-Knighten in Propria Persona.

The Court asked Ms. Knighten if she has obtained counsel. Ms. Knighten responded that she has not, that she has been working with Ms. Verzosa, that she was asked to file a supplement and gave a copy to Ms. Verzosa but failed to actually file the document (copy given to the court for review). The Court stated that Ms. Knighten needs to hire an attorney, that it will continue the matter one more time and that without an attorney this will be difficult to get through. Ms. Knighten stated that she did not look for an attorney as she was dealing with Ms. Verzosa. The Court stated that Ms. Verzosa is not of the opinion that this is going along. Ms. Knighten stated that she obtained all of the documents requested but failed to file them. The Court stated that there will be one more continuance, that Ms. Knighten needs to make an effort to hire an attorney, that the petition is incomplete and some things are incorrect, gave examples of the inconsistencies and stated that Ms. Knighten will not be appointed without counsel. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary was continued to September 19, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Aug-01-2016 Continued To Aug-29-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Yvonne Ennon-Knighten in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that the petition is incomplete and incorrect and that Ms. Knighten needs an attorney. Ms. Knighten stated that she is using a paralegal that has all of the necessary documents. The Court stated that the petition is incomplete with incorrect statements. Ms. Knighten stated that the paralegal says that everything requested has been done. The Court stated that there are items that are not filled out, that there is proof of a holographic instrument but no copy of the will attached, that the petition states that the petitioner is named executor in the will but that is not correct, that the petition stated that the will says no bond required but the will does not say that and that it can either continue the petition for an amendment or deny the petition without prejudice. Ms. Knighten stated that she would like a continuance to September, that she has been speaking with Ms. Verzosa but she is on vacation, that the paralegal has been submitting all of the documents and that she has a supplement that the paralegal told her to bring to court today. The Court continued the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary to August 29, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 stating that petitioner needs to hire an attorney. Ms. Knighted responded that she cannot afford an attorney and that is why she used a paralegal. The Court stated that what the paralegal has filed is not correct. Ms. Knighten as for documentation of the missing items, that the examiner's notes said what was missing and she has the supplement with her, that she is not sure if the paralegal received the notes and confirmed that she personally did not receive the notes. Ms. Knighten was referred to room 202 to speak with the examiner regarding obtaining a copy of the notes.

Judge: Paul Alvarado Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Saephan (Not Reported)
